title: Monitored-sources change-watch (FNS/CMS/IRS/state portals + Federal Register) → auto-opens re-verification tasks
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. 2025-2027 is an exceptional period of regulatory churn: the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA, signed 2025-07-04) rewrote SNAP and Medicaid work requirements with staggered effective dates (2026-10-01 and 2026-12-31/2027-01-01), and a DHS public-charge NPRM is pending. A calendar-only re-verification cadence is insufficient; the provenance model requires event-driven re-verification that fires when an authoritative source changes, not just on a schedule. This task implements that monitored-sources change-watch as an M4 hardening artifact, with the monitored-sources list configurable by the maintainer. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured.
objective: Implement a monitored-sources change-watch that polls a configurable list of official source URLs (FNS/CMS/IRS/state portals + Federal Register) for content changes and automatically opens re-verification tasks for every guide claim sourced from a changed page, making currency event-driven rather than calendar-only.
acceptanceCriteria: Implements a configurable change-watch that monitors official source URLs (FNS/CMS/IRS/state portals + Federal Register) for content changes, keyed to the sources recorded in the provenance layer (packages/provenance)
When a watched source changes, automatically surfaces a re-verification task for every claim in the corpus that cites that source, so no affected claim silently continues to serve as current

Integrates with the staleness fail-safe in packages/provenance: a triggered re-verification suspends the affected claims (auto-flag or withhold per severity) until the maintainer re-verifies and re-signs-off

The watch list is maintainer-configurable (add/remove sources without code changes); false-positive handling distinguishes cosmetic page changes from regulatory content changes; watch runs in CI and/or as a scheduled job
Candidate for extraction as a standalone currency-watch utility (documented extension point) that other civic-info projects could subscribe to, consistent with PLAN.md Adjacent Opportunities
